summary:
The patient likely has Graves’ disease causing thyrotoxicosis during pregnancy, supported by symptomatology including insomnia, palpitations, fatigue, and laboratory evidence of high free thyroid hormone levels and suppressed TSH. Propylthiouracil (PTU) should be initiated as first-line therapy because its reduced passage into maternal-fetal circulation lowers neonatal exposure relative to methimazole.

The patient most likely has Graves’ disease leading to thyrotoxicosis during pregnancy based on symptoms like palpitations, insomnia, fatigue, and abnormal labs (low TSH/high FT4). First-line treatment involves using propylthiouracil (PTU), chosen over other antithyroids due to less transplacentally transferred activity reducing potential harm to the fetus.
